visible_text_full:
ANDREJ KARPATHY
How LLMs Turn Raw Research
Into a Living Knowledge Base
Collect sources. Let the LLM organize them. Ask questions. Every answer makes the wiki smarter.

CORE INSIGHT
You never write the wiki. The LLM writes everything. You just steer — and every answer compounds.

MAIN PIPELINE
STEP 01
Collect Sources
Dump articles, papers, repos, datasets, and
images into a raw/ folder. Use Obsidian Web
Clipper to save articles as markdown with
images downloaded locally.

● Obsidian Web Clipper for articles
● Save related images locally
● arXiv, GitHub, datasets, repos

INDEX

STEP 02
LLM Organizes Wiki
The LLM reads everything in raw/ and builds a
structured wiki of .md files — summaries,
concept articles, and links between related
ideas across all your sources.

● Auto-generated index file
● Concept articles per topic
● Links between related ideas

ORGANIZE

STEP 03
Ask Questions
Query the wiki with complex questions. The
LLM reads its own index files and finds
answers across your entire collection. No
complex retrieval system needed.

● ~100 articles / ~400K words
● LLM maintains its own index
● Multi-step research queries

QUERY - RENDER - FILE BACK

STEP 04
Render Outputs
Outputs go beyond text — the LLM renders markdown
files, Marp slide decks, Matplotlib charts, and images.
All viewable directly inside Obsidian.

● Markdown articles
● Marp slide decks
● Matplotlib charts and plots

RENDER

STEP 05
File Back to Wiki
Every output gets filed back into the wiki. Every
question you ask makes the knowledge base smarter.
The loop compounds — your research builds on itself.

● Outputs go back to wiki/
● Knowledge compounds over time
● The loop never stops

Filed Back — Knowledge Compounds
This is the real insight. The wiki grows from both sides: what you save AND what you ask. Every interaction makes the system smarter. This is a second brain that builds itself.

SUPPORT LAYER

Obsidian (Frontend)
View raw data, the compiled wiki, and visualizations all in one
place. The human reads here. The LLM writes here. Marp
plugin renders slide decks.

Lint + Heal
The LLM runs "health checks" — finds bad data, fills gaps via
web search, suggests new connections and articles. The wiki
cleans and improves itself.

Search + CLI Tools
Vibe coded search engine over the wiki. Web UI for direct use.
CLI interface so the LLM can search its own knowledge base
as a tool for bigger queries.

LOOKING AHEAD
Train a Custom Model on Wiki Data
The next step: train the LLM on the research itself so it knows the material in its weights —
not just in the context window. Knowledge becomes permanent, not just referenced every
session.

STARTER PROMPT - PASTE INTO ANY AGENT
Try This Workflow Yourself
# Point at any folder of research
Read everything in /raw and organize
into a wiki of .md files in /wiki.
Create an index.md with summaries
and links between related concepts.
When I ask questions, use the wiki
to answer. File responses back so
it grows with every query.
Run a health check: find gaps,
fix errors, suggest new links.

Architecture based on @karpathy's workflow 11M views 42K likes April 2026
summary: This document outlines a five-step workflow for leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) to transform raw research into a dynamic, self-improving "living knowledge base." The process involves LLMs collecting, organizing, querying, rendering, and filing back information, with each interaction making the wiki smarter. The core idea is that the LLM handles the content creation, while the user guides the process, leading to a continuously growing and refined knowledge system.
